title: Add help text to all takt subcommands
description:
The plain 'takt' command (no subcommand) currently shows a usage block where only 'init' has a one-line description. All other subcommands (plan, run, bead, handoff, retry, merge, summary, tui, telemetry) show no description at all.

Add a brief one-line help string to every subcommand parser so the top-level usage block looks like:

  {plan,run,bead,handoff,retry,merge,summary,tui,telemetry,init}
    plan                Plan a spec file into a bead graph
    run                 Run the scheduler (one cycle or continuous)
    bead                Manage beads (create, list, show, graph, delete, ...)
    handoff             Show handoff summary for a bead
    retry               Requeue a blocked bead
    merge               Merge a completed feature branch into main
    summary             Show bead counts and next actionable beads
    tui                 Open the interactive terminal UI
    telemetry           Show telemetry data for a bead
    init                Initialise a new project for orchestration

Also fix the prog name shown in the usage line: it currently says 'orchestrator' but should say 'takt'.

The relevant file is src/agent_takt/cli.py. Each subcommand is added via add_parser(); pass a help= string to each call. The prog name is set in the top-level ArgumentParser constructor.

Acceptance criteria:
- Running 'takt' with no arguments (or 'takt --help') shows a one-line description next to every subcommand.
- The usage line reads 'usage: takt ...' not 'usage: orchestrator ...'.
- All existing tests pass.

handoff_summary.completed: Updated ArgumentParser prog to 'takt' and added help= strings to all 9 subcommand parsers (plan, run, bead, handoff, retry, merge, summary, tui, telemetry). The init parser already had a help string. Verified output with python -c import matches the acceptance criteria exactly.
handoff_summary.risks: None — purely additive metadata change to argparse parser construction.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester should verify 'takt --help' and 'takt' with no args produce the expected output. All existing tests should continue to pass since this is a non-behavioral change.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — only cli.py was modified and no other in-progress beads share this file.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Used argparse's built-in help= parameter on each add_parser() call — the idiomatic way to add subcommand descriptions to the top-level usage block. No alternatives were needed since this is the standard argparse pattern. The prog= fix on the top-level ArgumentParser is a one-line change with no trade-offs.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tester should: (1) run 'takt' with no arguments and confirm all subcommands show one-line descriptions; (2) run 'takt --help' and verify usage line reads 'usage: takt ...' not 'usage: orchestrator ...'; (3) confirm existing test suite still passes. No new code paths were added — this is purely metadata on parser construction.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: N/A — implementation is complete as specified.

conflict_risks: None — only cli.py was modified and no other in-progress beads share this file.

execution_history.summary: Added help text to all takt subcommands and fixed prog name from 'orchestrator' to 'takt' in src/agent_takt/cli.py.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
